rulings.Case Title: EDDIE CHUEN-LEUNG CHEUNG M.D VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Petition For Writ
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2021, Line 1, PETITIONER EDDIE CHEUNG's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Petition For Writ. Plaintiff's timely motion for reconsideration is granted pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1008. (The Court's full tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) Effective Monday, June 28, 2021, for the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, attorneys and parties may appear either in person in Department 302 or remotely by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: VINCE MORECI VS. FULTON STREET CONSTRUCTION INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Vince Morecis Further Responses To Stockham Construction, Inc.S Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2021, Line 1, CROSS-COMPLAINANT STOCKHAM CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Vince Moreci's Further Responses To Stockham Construction, Inc.'s Discovery Requests: Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is denied. Discovery is closed per the Court's February 2021 Order, which set a new trial date and a discovery deadline. No subsequent order has changed that. No sanctions. For the 9:00a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREY KUKUSHKIN VS. MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Portions Of The Fifth Amended Complaint [Ccp 436]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2021, Line 4, 2- Motion To Strike Portions Of The Fifth Amended Complaint [CCP 436] By Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc., Misha Breyburg, And Sasha Plotitsa. Defendant Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc.'s motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to the request for attorney's fees. Plaintiff does not plead any legal, statutory, or contractual basis to recover fees in his individual capacity. Plaintiff's cases regarding private attorney general fees are inapposite. If plaintiff subsequently discovers a basis for fees, he may seek leave to amend. The remainder of the motion to strike is denied. The promissory fraud claim supports the request for exemplary damages. The individual claims are well-pleaded so the first basis for the motion to strike fails. Given the minor deficiency that the notice of errata was attempting to cure, there is no purpose in striking the 5th Amended Complaint as the Court would grant leave to amend and plaintiff would be able to re-file the exact same pleading as the 6th Amended Complaint. "The law neither does nor requires idle acts." (Civ. Code Sec. 3532.) Plaintiff shall obtain leave of court for future amendments to the complaint, if any. Effective Monday, June 28, 2021, for the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, attorneys and parties may appear either in person in Department 302 or remotely by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A WAERDT PHD VS. THE HARCOURT GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatores, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions (Added To Calendar For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2021, Line 4, (Part 2 of 2 of the Tentative Ruling) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatores, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions (added for tentative ruling entry purposes only): Here, further responses to many of the form interrogatories would serve no practical benefit to Defendant. The exception is the response to Interrogatories 6.1-6.6 and 10.1-10.3. Since Plaintiff is claiming emotional distress damages, Defendant is entitled to inquire as to Plaintiff's medical history in this regard. It is not clear that the complaint contains any cause of action which would support a claim for lost income. Sanctions are awarded to Defendant in the amount of $2,310. For the 9:00a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. (End of Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A WAERDT PHD VS. THE HARCOURT GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatores, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2021, Line 4, DEFENDANT SOJOURN PROPERTIES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatores, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions (Part 1 of 2): Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part, as follows. It appears that Plaintiff has made a reasonable attempt to answer most of these form interrogatories. Regardless, he is required to serve a signed verification within 10 days. As noted in Greyhound Corp. v. Superior Court (1961) 56 Cal.2d 355, 383-384, "In the exercise of its discretion the court should weigh the relative importance of the information sought against the hardship which its production might entail...." The court should not order a further response where "the disclosure of matters so remote from the subject matter of the action as disclosed by the issues framed by the pleadings [] make[s] their disclosure of little or no practical benefit to the party seeking the disclosure." Ryan v. Superior Court (1960) 186 Cal. App.2d 813, 817. (Tentative Ruling Continued, See Next Entry) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AARDVARK STORAGE UNLIMITED, INC. DBA AMERICAN VS. DANIEL M. KIFOR
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2021, Line 8, DEFENDANT DANIEL KIFOR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with Code Civ. Proc. section 430.41, which requires counsel to meet and confer in person or by telephone before a demurrer is filed. Counsel Brian Brophy's declaration indicates this did not occur. All counsel are ordered to comply with Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.41. Defendants' response to the complaint is now due by December 2, 2021. =(302/EPS)
